claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period; Child has started an apprenticeship in England
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions. Child Benefit eligibility is determined separately for each child, so I'll need information about any children you're asking about.

**First question: Do you have a child (or children) that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me how many children you'd like me to assess?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child I'd like to ask about. My son Alex is 17 years old and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 17 years old, I need to check a few more details about his situation.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** 

This would include things like:
- School or college courses
- A-levels or similar qualifications
- Approved vocational training

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's just started an apprenticeship, actually. He's in training for a trade.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. An apprenticeship can be considered approved training for Child Benefit purposes.

**Is Alex working 24 hours a week or more (excluding the training element of his apprenticeship)?** 

In other words, I need to know if his paid work hours (not counting the college or training days that are part of the apprenticeship) are 24 hours or more per week.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't work 24 hours a week or more. The apprenticeship includes both the training and work components, but he's not doing additional paid work on top of that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I need to clarify one important detail about Alex's apprenticeship. **Where does Alex live - is this in England, Scotland, or Wales?**

This matters because the rules for apprenticeships differ depending on location:
- In England, all types of apprenticeships exclude someone from Child Benefit
- In Scotland, Modern Apprenticeships exclude someone from Child Benefit  
- In Wales, only Foundation Apprenticeships may qualify

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I live in England - we're based in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I now have enough information to make a determination about your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Unfortunately, based on the information you've provided, **you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

**Alex's situation:**
- He is 17 years old (within the 16-19 age range where education/training is assessed)
- He is doing an apprenticeship in England

**The key rule:** In England, all types of apprenticeships (including Intermediate, Advanced, Higher, and Degree Apprenticeships) exclude someone from qualifying for Child Benefit. This is because apprenticeships are considered employer-funded training with an agreement for future employment.

Even though Alex isn't working 24 hours or more per week separately from his apprenticeship, the apprenticeship itself means he doesn't meet the criteria for being in "approved education or training" for Child Benefit purposes.

**What this means:** Child Benefit would have stopped being payable for Alex once he started his apprenticeship, even though he's under 20 years old.

I'm sorry this isn't the outcome you were hoping for. If Alex's circumstances change in the future (for example, if he leaves the apprenticeship and enters approved education), you may become eligible at that point.

Is there anything else you'd like me to clarify about this decision?
